2605.21135.title: Smarter edits? Post-editing with error highlights and translation suggestions
2605.21135.paper_summary: As MT quality increases, interest in enhanced post-editing features such as QE-derived error highlights is growing, yet evidence for their usefulness remains limited. In this work, we explore the usefulness of LLM-derived error highlights and correction suggestions based on automatic post-editing (APE). We conduct a study where professional translators (En-Nl) post-edit translations using APE error highlights and correction suggestions and compare productivity, quality and user experience to regular PE and PE with QE-derived highlights. While no condition yielded productivity or quality gains compared to regular PE, APE highlights were better received than QE-derived highlights, and correction suggestions improved overall user experience.
2605.21135.paper_summary_zh: 隨著機器翻譯質量的提高，對於增強後編輯功能的興趣也在增長，例如基於質量評估（QE）衍生的錯誤高亮，但其有效性的證據仍然有限。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們探索基於自動後編輯（APE）的LLM衍生錯誤高亮和修正建議的有用性。<lb/>我們進行了一項研究，專業翻譯人員（英荷語）使用APE錯誤高亮和修正建議進行後編輯，並將生產力、質量和用戶體驗與常規後編輯及帶有QE衍生高亮的後編輯進行比較。<lb/>儘管沒有任何條件相比於常規後編輯帶來生產力或質量的提升，但APE高亮的接受度優於QE衍生的高亮，而修正建議改善了整體用戶體驗。
2605.21135.author: Fleur V. J. van Tellingen et.al.
2605.21135.authors: Fleur V. J. van Tellingen, Gautam Ranka, Dora Žugčić, Joyce van der Wal, Andrea Camasta, Livio Guerra, Alina Karakanta
